title: runtime segfault with containers in created state
body:
off of latest master, I got a segfault trying to run ps
```$ ./bin/podman ps -a                                                                                                                                         
panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference                                                                                       
[signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x28 pc=0x12db03d]                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                              
goroutine 1 [running]:                                                                                                                                        
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.(*storageService).GetRunDir(0xc000028ae0, 0xc000049740, 0x40, 0x0, 0xc000141da8, 0xc000141db0, 0xc0003e8a48)
        libpod/storage.go:305 +0x2d                
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.(*Container).refresh(0xc0005389a0, 0xc0000dd638, 0x1)
        libpod/container_internal.go:576 +0xa4                                                                                                                
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.(*Runtime).refresh(0xc0004325b0, 0xc000045ec0, 0x1f, 0x0, 0x0)
        libpod/runtime.go:1237 +0x17c                                         
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.makeRuntime(0x1bb4fe0, 0xc0000d8018, 0xc0004325b0, 0x0, 0x0)
        libpod/runtime.go:1118 +0x14d1                             
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.newRuntimeFromConfig(0x1bb4fe0, 0xc0000d8018, 0x0, 0x0, 0xc000370520, 0x2, 0x2, 0xc0004325b0, 0x0, 0x0)
        libpod/runtime.go:664 +0x70c                       
github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.NewRuntime(...)                           
        libpod/runtime.go:393                                       
github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman/libpodruntime.getRuntime(0x1bb4fe0, 0xc0000d8018, 0x2c13620, 0x7010000, 0x3, 0xc0004d9d68, 0xc0004d9cf0)
        cmd/podman/libpodruntime/runtime.go:167 +0xbdf              
github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman/libpodruntime.GetRuntimeNoStore(...)                                                                                  
        cmd/podman/libpodruntime/runtime.go:33                                 
github.com/containers/libpod/pkg/adapter.GetRuntimeNoStore(0x1bb4fe0, 0xc0000d8018, 0x2c13620, 0x21, 0xc00047b200, 0xc0004d9d80)
        pkg/adapter/runtime.go:64 +0x50                    
main.psCmd(0x2c13620, 0x0, 0x0)                                                                                                                               
        cmd/podman/ps.go:163 +0x464                                 
main.glob..func10(0x2ba4640, 0xc000370400, 0x0, 0x1, 0x0, 0x0)                 
        cmd/podman/ps.go:109 +0x86                                             
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).execute(0x2ba4640, 0xc0000c8170, 0x1, 0x1, 0x2ba4640, 0xc0000c8170)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:826 +0x465                    
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).ExecuteC(0x2badec0, 0xc0000c8d20, 0x7ffdf4ca6a2c, 0xc)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:914 +0x2fc                    
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra.(*Command).Execute(...)
        vendor/github.com/spf13/cobra/command.go:864                           
main.main()                            
        cmd/podman/main.go:148 +0x88          
```

comments.body:
original description was not correct.

I ran the ps after no activity for a while. I only had two containers in the created state. After this segfault, I successsfully created a new container, and ps works after that. Something about the initial refresh was funky.

`ps` can now optionally no longer create a store (does this by default for speed in IO constrained situations). This obviously causes refresh, which needs a store, to crash. I was pretty sure I fixed this one earlier, but evidently not - will look tomorrow.

Reproducer:

* Have at least 1 container created. Ensure all containers are stopped with `podman stop -a`.
* `sudo rm -f /dev/shm/libpod_lock /var/run/libpod/alive`
* `sudo ./bin/podman ps -a`
